vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes the mode of /etc/gshadow, a standard STIG file permission adjustment. The target mode (0000) is the default secure setting and does not affect system boot, networking, or critical services. The change is low‑risk and reversible.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Incorrect ownership could prevent group password operations; If the file is in use during the change, a brief read error could occur
